MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: MICHAEL S. KECK OF THE LAW OFFICES OF C.L. KECK, 415-861-2267, FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, DANIEL HERRERA AND ERIC HERRERA

ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING.
DEFENDANTS DANIEL HERRERA AND ERIC HERRERA'S Motion To Set Aside Void Default And Default Judgment -- GRANTED; ALL AWARDS OF FEES AND COSTS ARE SET ASIDE BECAUSE THEY WERE GRANTED IN EXCESS OF COURT'S JURISDICTION. JUDGE, PAUL H. ALVARADO, ROBERT BALIAN, CSR #5220 (PHA/301/AC)

Motion For Order Confirming Sale Or Real Property
Off Calendar Jul-22-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christy J. Styer, Esq. of MICHAEL B. BASSI, A LAW CORP. 986-8122, (Court Appointed Referee), moving to confirm sale of Real Property
Michael S. Keck, Esq. of LAW OFFICES of C.L. KECK 861-2267, for defendants Daniel Herrera and Eric Herrera, opposing the motion
Ramiro Castro, Esq., 885-1788, for plaintiff Oscar D. Herrera did not oppose to the motion

RULING:
* The Court adopted its tentative ruling: REFEREE CHRISTY J. STYER'S Motion For Order Confirming Sale Or Real Property -- (1) Granted confirmation of sale on certain conditions; (2) Referee's request for fees was granted in the amount of $17,608 plus amount not to exceed $3,000 covering the rest of the proceedings; (3) Request for Referee's attorney's fees was granted in the amount of $1,380; (4) Request for 6% Broker's commission was granted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
** Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees was granted.
*** All attorneys' fees and expense will be paid by the defaulting parties. Referee is to prepare an additonal order. (REQ/301/DR) Reporter: BELDON YEE, CSR #2647

Order Defendant Daniel B. Herrera To Show Cause And Temporary Restraining Order - Rents, Issues And Profits
Off Calendar Dec-09-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christy J. Styer, Esq. of MICHAEL B. BASSI 986-8122, COURT APPOINTED REFEREE, moving for defendant Daniel B. Herrera to show cause and temporary restraining order
Michael S. Keck, Esq. of LAW OFFICES OF C. L. KECK 861-2267, for defendant Daniel B. Herrera
* A R G U E D and the Court adopted the referee's requests:
1. Defendant to turn in the keys of the property and individual units by 5 p.m. tomorrow to the Referee. A locksmith will be hired if keys are not returned.
2. A 24-hour notice will be posted on the door of the building today. A real estate broker will view the building and individual units tomorrow at 5 p.m. Any tenants not available will be notified individually by posting notices on the doors.
3. A list of all tenants and residents will be delivered by the defendant to the Referee by
5 p.m. tomorrow.
4. Defendant will provide an accounting of rents for the months of October, November and December by 12/23/02 to the Referee.
5. The Referee is to prepare an order.
(JJM/301) Reporter: Joe Vickstein CSR # 4780

Order Defendant Daniel B. Herrera To Show Cause And Temporary Restraining Order - Rents, Issues And Profits

MINI MINUTES:

Defendant Daniel B. Herrera's Order to show cause and temporary restraining order-- C O N T I N U E D T O December 9, 2002 per stipulation of the parties. (JJM/301/DR) Reporter: Eric Throne CSR # 7855

Order Defendant Daniel B. Herrera To Show Cause And Temporary Restraining Order - Rents, Issues And Profits
Off Calendar Nov-27-2002 Continued To Dec-04-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Christy J. Styer, COURT APPOINTED REFEREE 986-8122-X710, moving for Order to show cause and temporary restraining order
No other appearance. The Court ordered Referee's petition for an Order for defendant Daniel B. Herrera to show cause and temporary restraining order C O N T I N U E D to
December 4, 2002 at 9:30 a.m. The Court extended the temporary restraining order through and including 12/4/2002. Moving party to notice defendant Daniel Herrera about the 12/4/2002 hearing and to present the proofs of service for both hearings (11/27, 12/4) to the hearing. Moving party is to prepare an order.
